title: Chaenorhinum lapeyrouseanum
intro: Chaenorhinum lapeyrouseanum, commonly known as Mediterranean toadflax, is a flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Chaenorhinum lapeyrouseanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also dried and used in capsules or tablets for its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Chaenorhinum lapeyrouseanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It belongs to the genus Chaenorhinum and is a member of the order Lamiales. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Chaenorhinum lapeyrouseanum is a small, annual herb that produces small, white or pink flowers. It is often considered a rare or endangered species due to habitat loss and fragmentation.
